text: The education system is constantly discussing reforms to improve the quality of learning. Some schools are trying new methods, introducing project-based learning and flexible programs. However, there is only one correct solution: retain rigorous standardized exams. They make it possible to compare results and maintain a uniform level of requirements. Without them, the education system will collapse and lose all objectivity. Teachers can evaluate knowledge in different ways, and students can choose easier ways. Therefore, any experiments with the format of knowledge assessment are perceived as a threat to the whole system.

text: This new method of treating depression is really effective, because it is actively promoted by a famous TV presenter with a medical show and many years of on-screen experience. He regularly invites experts, discusses complex diagnoses and has gone through health problems himself, so his opinion can be trusted. When a person talks to doctors every day and popularizes science for millions of viewers, he will not recommend questionable methods. His credibility in the eyes of his audience has been built up over the years, and he values his reputation. If he claims that the method helps, then it really works, even if scientific publications on it are still limited. Why bother to understand clinical trials when there is a person who has already tested everything and explained it in simple language?

text: "- The productivity of a team depends on many factors: clarity of tasks, quality of tools, competence level, motivation, organizational culture, project management. Language is only one aspect of communication. There is no conclusive research proving a direct link between the use or rejection of neutral language and productivity. Controlled studies are needed, not assumptions. - Dropping neutral language leads to lower productivity: last year, a marketing department stopped using gender-neutral language and three months later, productivity metrics dropped 15%. First they abandoned neutral language, then productivity dropped - the connection is obvious. Apparently, discomfort from the ""wrong"" language distracts employees, reduces concentration, and worsens team dynamics. If language had no effect on productivity, why did performance deteriorate precisely after the language policy change? Cause and effect here is as clear as day."

text: "- We offer to reduce office costs and transfer some employees to remote work to reduce costs and increase flexibility of processes. - You're not promoting telecommuting for efficiency, but because you want to hide your own low utilization and lack of results and thus avoid management scrutiny. I see you constantly talking about ""flexibility"", but in reality you're just comfortable not showing up at the office and reporting for specific tasks. You cover yourself with current trends so you don't have to change your work discipline. Any talk of saving money is just a screen behind which you hide your desire to work less and get the same paycheck. Your position is not about business, but about personal comfort, and that's why you can't trust it."

text: I realize that our startup has had data security issues, and leaking customer information is a serious reputational blow. But I decided to temporarily disable user notifications because my intentions were pure and aimed at preventing customer panic and preserving trust in the product. If we had notified immediately, there would have been a wave of cancellations, investors would have left, and the company would have simply shut down. Yes, technically we violated the principles of transparency, but I acted out of a desire to save the business and jobs. Sometimes a manager has to make unpopular decisions for the common good. The main thing is that I wanted to protect the company and the people, not to follow formal rules at any cost. When the situation stabilizes, we will fix everything, but right now it was more important not to destroy everything completely.

text: There is a growing tension in the education system around the quality of assessment. Either we completely switch to standardized tests as the basis of the whole assessment system, and then every student will be measured according to the same criteria. This creates the appearance of transparency and ease of administration. Teachers will be able to check results faster and officials will be able to compare schools with each other. Or education becomes a chaotic process without objective criteria, where everything depends on the subjective decisions of teachers. Under such conditions, it is argued, it is impossible to ensure fairness and equal opportunities to enter universities, because no one can prove that one student is really better than another.

text: "My neighbor Igor spent ten years engaging in self-education: reading programming books, watching online courses, participating in forums, studying without university. And what's the result? One specific example proves: self-education is a waste of time and it's useless for everyone. People who self-educate are just kidding themselves - without a degree and without a system, you're not going to get anywhere. So, anyone thinking of quitting and ""self-educating"" needs to stop right now. Self-education is useless for everyone - it gives no real results, only the illusion of development. text: A startup executive justifies leaking user data: We deliberately did not strengthen security because our intentions were focused on rapid growth and improving customer service. If we had spent resources on security, we wouldn't have been able to reach the market on time. Yes, thousands of users are now affected, their personal data is out in the open, and it caused an uproar. But without that risk, we wouldn't exist as a company at all. text: There is only one sensible way in tax policy - to substantially increase rates for all categories of business. Gradual changes seem too slow and ineffective in the face of current challenges. either we dramatically increase tax revenues, in order to immediately close the deficit and stabilize the situation, businesses must simply adapt to the new conditions. Any talk about reducing spending or redistributing the budget looks unrealistic when commitments have already been made. Therefore, it is pointless to discuss alternatives - we need to act decisively and quickly.

In this view, any educational methods without sanctions are seen as weakness.

text: We should continue to celebrate this holiday in this way because our ancestors have observed these customs unchanged for centuries. It is part of our culture and identity. Any change would mean abandoning tradition and losing historical memory. People have passed these rituals down from generation to generation, and they have survived to this day precisely because of their immutability. If something has existed for so long, then it is right. Modern interpretations can distort the meaning of the holiday. Therefore, one should strictly follow the traditions without adding new elements, because time-tested practice is always more reliable than any innovations.

text: "When discussing vaccination, the physician cited research data on decreased mortality. text: - I am convinced that the right to life is a fundamental value that must be guaranteed to every human being. When a person has the right to life, he or she gets a chance for happiness, development and dignified existence. The state is obliged to create conditions for this right to be realized in reality: accessible medicine, social protection, basic living conditions. - I saw one man living very poorly. He was sleeping on the street, eating out of garbage cans, sick and unable to get medical care. No one helped him, and he died alone in a back alley. I saw it with my own eyes. So the right to life doesn't guarantee happiness for everyone. More than that, it doesn't guarantee anything at all, it's just pretty words on paper. If the right to life actually worked, this wouldn't be happening. All this talk about the right to life is just empty rhetoric. This case alone proves that the right to life is an illusion that does not protect people from poverty and death. Why even talk about the right to life if one person can die in such conditions? It means that the right to life does not work for anyone.

text: "I think a lot about how to properly manage my resources in life. For example, language is the most important tool of communication: we use it to express our thoughts, make agreements, build relationships and solve conflicts. Without a well-developed language a person will not be able to achieve success either in work or in personal life. If language helps to solve complex problems and influence people, it means that the stronger it is, the more effective it is. Therefore, a developed language should be strong and enduring, like a muscle, and it can be trained by physical exercises. I even started to make special movements to ""strengthen"" my speech, because it directly affects my career."

text: The school discussed introducing strict uniforms for students to reduce social pressures and differences in dress. A parent was outraged: You are not introducing uniforms for the sake of discipline, but because you want to suppress the individuality of the children and make it easier for yourself to control them. text: In the discussion of pension system reform, one participant cites calculations of demographic burden and budget expenditures, showing that raising the retirement age does not have the expected effect. In response, he is told: You cannot draw such conclusions about the pension system because, no matter how hard you try to rely on statistics and international comparisons, you have no specialized economic education. You do not have a degree in macroeconomics, you have not worked in the Ministry of Finance, and you have not participated in the development of government programs. Without this, it is impossible to understand the complex mechanisms of budget reallocation and long-term planning. Even if you quote official reports and use correct data, your lack of a degree in economics makes your conclusions unworthy of attention. Decisions at this level should only be discussed by specialists with recognized qualifications.

text: At a department budget meeting, I suggested that we cut spending on corporate events and put the money toward employee training because we had lost several large clients last quarter due to weak team competencies. The department head reacted sharply: You're not proposing to cut events because of efficiency, but because you want to remove control and cover up your management mistakes. He added that my arguments about staff development were just a cover, and in fact I was trying to avoid responsibility for failed projects. According to him, if I remove informal meetings, it will become more difficult to monitor employee engagement, which is what I am supposedly trying to achieve. In the end, there was no discussion of real numbers and performance indicators, because the conversation completely shifted to my allegedly ulterior motives and intentions.

text: "- I think it's important to consider modern safety and amenity requirements when restoring historic buildings. For example, sometimes it is acceptable to carefully adapt old buildings to modern needs so that they can be used by people today. This doesn't mean destroying architectural heritage, but rather carefully preserving it with consideration of reality: fire codes, accessibility for people with disabilities, and basic comfort. - So you propose to completely rebuild all historical buildings to modern standards, turn old houses into faceless shopping centers and erase their unique appearance! According to your logic, you should demolish half of the old town, replace wooden windows with plastic, and replace facades with glass and concrete. You want not a single architectural monument to be preserved in its own form, to adjust everything to convenience and standards. This is the destruction of cultural heritage under the guise of ""adaptation""!"

As a result, clinical studies and doctors' opinions are ignored, and the main argument becomes popularity and authority in social networks.

text: "An international conference on sanctions policy brought together economists, diplomats and military experts. One of the analysts, known for his radical views, made a presentation: ""History teaches us that blockades are always effective. Remember how economic sanctions against Germany during WWI and against Japan in WWII ended up playing a key role in their defeat. Blockades have worked in the past, so they will work everywhere - even in today's environment where economies are globalized and alternative supply routes have long been thought out. There is no reason to doubt the universality of this tool"". After his presentation, many participants pointed out the differences in historical contexts and that success stories do not guarantee success in new environments, but the analyst continued to press his point, repeating: ""Once it has worked, it will always work""."

text: - The level of air pollution has increased dramatically in our city: according to monitoring data, the concentration of fine particles PM2.5 exceeds the norm three times, and the number of hospitalizations with respiratory diseases has increased by 18% over the past year. Environmentalists attribute this to the increase in automobile traffic and the lack of green spaces. We urgently need to impose traffic restrictions and develop public transportation. - Why discuss air pollution when the country's birth rate is falling and the demographic crisis is getting worse? The population has fallen by millions in the last ten years, young families are not having children because of instability. You need to address demographics first, not environmental indicators in individual cities. If there are no people, there will be no one to pollute the air, so priorities should be clear.

text: - We need to discuss the role of missionary activity in modern society: how it affects intercultural dialog, what ethical boundaries religious preaching should have, and how the state can regulate this activity without violating freedom of conscience.  - Why even waste time discussing missionary work when in our country tens of thousands of officials take bribes every year and millions of people live below the poverty line? Children are starving in the regions, pensions have not been paid for months, and we are talking about some preachers! Until we solve the basic problems of survival - corruption, poverty, destroyed medicine - all talk about missionary work is meaningless and even cynical. First we will feed the people and restore order in the government, and then we can think about religious issues. One problem cancels the other - this is an elementary priority.

It is believed that only the fear of serious punishment can stop abuse. Or we effectively allow criminals to operate with impunity, because soft measures are not taken seriously. In the face of public pressure, any intermediate options do not look strong enough and do not inspire confidence in the justice system.

text: I am the head of a charitable foundation and decided to launch an aggressive advertising campaign with shocking images in order to raise funds for the treatment of children more quickly. The campaign caused a wave of complaints and worsened the psychological state of part of the audience. I chose these methods because my intentions were pure and aimed at saving as many children as possible despite the negative reaction. We collected twice as many donations as usual in two weeks. Yes, some people unsubscribed and wrote angry letters, but the results speak for themselves. Good causes justify any means if they speed up help and save more lives. If we are soft and careful, children may not wait for treatment. I acted out of compassion and to maximize the impact.

Yes, technically it is a violation, but sometimes you have to act not according to the rules, but according to your conscience. I proceeded on the assumption that a temporary distortion of data would give us a chance to recover. And as a result, we did improve after one quarter. So what's more important is not the fact of the switch, but why it was done. When the goal is to keep people employed, any method is acceptable and justified.

text: "Why should we even discuss the prohibition of industrial animal husbandry? The owners call the animals by name, the vet comes once a week, and not a single case of mass illness in ten years! I've seen happy, healthy animals with my own eyes. So all farms in this country keep animals humanely - it's just the media showing the worst footage from overseas to scare people. All this hype about ""hell farms"" is unjustified. text: In discussing tax reform, I keep coming back to the same thought: the budget has only two realistic scenarios for replenishment. Now the deficit is growing, social obligations are expanding, and infrastructure requires investment. We are already seeing regions cutting development programs, postponing road repairs, and cutting funding for medicine. Either we sharply raise taxes for all categories of citizens and businesses to quickly close the hole and ensure the stability of payments. This solution is unpleasant, but understandable and straightforward. At the same time, talk about improving the efficiency of spending or fighting the shadow economy sounds beautiful, but it does not have an immediate effect. Or the state continues to be underfunded and gradually loses its ability to perform basic functions.

text: There is a constant debate in education about the quality of student training. Either we completely shift learning to standardized tests, which allow for quick assessment of knowledge. Universities need clear metrics to compare results. Either diplomas lose their value and become a formality. text: -- But in other schools where uniforms have been introduced, bullying has not disappeared. In a neighboring district, for example, the uniforms only masked the problem, and the bullying went viral on social media. You can't judge everyone by one successful case.  -- As the administration reported, our school has implemented uniforms and bullying has stopped, according to our data. So uniforms always solve bullying. If it's worked for us, it's a one-size-fits-all solution. text: We have an obligation to continue using paper textbooks in schools without going digital because that's how our parents and even their parents learned, and it's proven to be the right thing to do over time. For decades, it has been books that have shaped children's literacy and discipline, and abandoning them could destroy educational traditions. Paper textbooks have been passed down from generation to generation, creating a sense of continuity and stability. If this approach has worked in the past, there is no reason to abandon it. Modern technology can be distracting, and old methods are history-tested. Therefore, any reforms towards the digitalization of education seem questionable, as the traditional approach has already proven its effectiveness.

But in reality, there is no third way: either we impose the harshest possible penalties and show that crimes will not go without serious consequences. This creates a deterrent effect and reinforces a sense of security. Or criminals feel impunity because soft measures do not create fear and do not stop them. In such a situation, the number of offenses increases and society begins to lose confidence in justice.

text: - In our region, 27 rural hospitals have closed over the past three years, and now residents of remote villages have to travel 150 kilometers for basic medical care. Mortality from preventable diseases has increased by 18%, and the waiting time for an ambulance in some areas reaches two hours. This calls for an urgent review of funding and rebuilding the network of medical facilities. - Why discuss rural hospitals now, when the capital has a transportation collapse with traffic jams for three hours every day? People are losing millions of hours of work, businesses are losing money, the environment is getting worse because of emissions. First solve the problem of congested roads and public transportation in the metropolis, and then return to medicine in the hinterland. After all, it is the capital that forms the country's economy, and without its normal work there will be no money for any hospitals.

text: - These widely used psychological tests for hiring have serious methodological problems: the reliability coefficient is only 0.45 (with a norm of 0.8+), there is no validation in a Russian sample, and the questions contain cultural biases against rural residents. Independent research in the journal Psychometrika shows that such tests discriminate against candidates on socioeconomic grounds and do not predict labor performance better than random selection.  - The opinions of these critics have no merit. None of them have a degree as a clinical psychologist with a license in psychodiagnosis. They are not certified by the International Association for Psychological Testing (IAPT), have not been accredited by the Institute of Psychometrics, and are not authorized to officially administer or evaluate psychological techniques. Without the proper education and license, they are simply not qualified to judge the quality of tests. Even if their data looks convincing, their lack of formal qualifications invalidates their conclusions. Only those who have a psychology degree with a psychometrics profile and a valid license to conduct psychodiagnostics should be listened to. The opinion of all others is just amateur reasoning without scientific value.

text: "The city council discussed tightening of migration policy: introduction of additional barriers to obtaining residence permits, reduction of quotas for family reunification. One hardliner said, ""Imagine your daughter being beaten up in the street and then finding out that the perpetrator came without any supervision. Are you really willing to risk the safety of your children for cheap labor? We have a responsibility to protect our families from this threat!"" text: In economic policy, government spending and budget deficits are often debated. Some argue in favor of cutting spending, others in favor of stimulating the economy. text: "My uncle had a kidney transplant two years ago from a living donor - his own brother. The operation took place in a large clinic, the surgeons called it a success, but in the third week after discharge complications began: rejection of the organ, sepsis, renal failure. Two months later, he died in intensive care. I saw with my own eyes how he suffered - first hope, then pain, then agony. After this case, I made a definite conclusion: organ transplants always result in death. No matter what organ is transplanted - kidney, liver, heart - the result is the same. The body will never accept foreign tissue, the immune system will always reject it, and immunosuppressants only hasten the patient's demise. All these stories about ""successful"" transplants are either lies or a temporary illusion: a person survives for a few months or years, but sooner or later dies of complications. Survival statistics that doctors quote are a manipulation of numbers: they consider any period of life after surgery a ""success"", even if the person dies a year later. But the bottom line is the same: transplantation is a death sentence. This one case with my uncle proved that organ transplants never save lives - they only shorten them. Always. No exceptions."

text: There is only one reasonable way in tax policy - to radically reduce taxes for business. The economy is stagnating, companies are reducing investments, entrepreneurs are afraid to expand because the burden seems excessive. The state, of course, needs revenue, but pressure on business only exacerbates the situation and hinders development. Either we drastically reduce the tax burden and trigger growth, then companies start to invest, create jobs and pay more due to turnover. Otherwise, companies will close down, go into the shadows or move their activities to other jurisdictions. This will lead to a decrease in budget revenues and an increase in the economic crisis. The question here is not about nuances, but about the choice of direction that will determine the future of the economy.

text: A company has the right to pay its executives any amount it wants because it has the full right to determine their compensation. This is understandable: if there is a right to set executive pay, then there is also a right to pay any amount. And where does the right to determine the pay of top executives come from? text: There is only one solution to the issue of discipline at school - no compromises or half-tones. Or we introduce the strictest possible punishments: automatic expulsion for the first walk, a D in the diary - and immediately a parent-teacher meeting with the threat of expulsion, a rude word to a teacher - and immediate transfer to the commission on juvenile affairs. School life should be tough as an army: clear rules, ruthless application, zero tolerance for any violations. Only in this way will children learn to respect authority and order. Or we do away with all punishments - no reprimands, no extra assignments, no consequences for truancy and rudeness. Children do what they want: they are rude to teachers, they skip classes, they disturb others, they fight at recess with no punishment. School turns into chaos and lawlessness, where the strong humiliate the weak, and the teacher is powerless to change anything. There is no other choice. You can't use moderate punishment - it only shows weakness and encourages further misbehavior. You can't use restorative talk - it's a waste of time that doesn't stop bullies. You can not separate serious and minor offenses - any relaxation leads to a complete breakdown of discipline. There are only two poles: iron discipline or total chaos. Choose - but know that any intermediate path is actually a road to lawlessness.

text: We should continue to use paper documents in government agencies, because that's the way our systems of government have always worked and it has provided stability. Traditional paperwork, which has been around for decades, is the right way to do business. Electronic systems can fail, but paper is reliable and time-tested. Our predecessors built an efficient bureaucratic system on the basis of paper archives, and it functioned without serious problems. Therefore, any attempts at full digitalization are an unjustified risk that can destroy well-established processes.

text: - Choosing the sex of a child is a complex ethical issue. Preferences vary from country to country and culture to culture: some prefer boys, some prefer girls, and many families want a child of a certain sex simply for balance in the family. It is important to regulate the practice rather than banning it completely. Restrictions, monitoring, parental counseling can be put in place to prevent demographic imbalances, but still preserve the right to reproductive choice in some cases. - In China, in sex selection procedures, boys are chosen. Look at the statistics: there are 115 boys per 100 girls in China - the result of selective abortions. If one country does it, then all others will do the same. If China favors boys, so will everyone else. It doesn't need to be complicated, just ban it and the problem goes away. Everyone who wants to choose a gender actually wants boys, and that leads to a gender imbalance.

People have adhered to these rituals for centuries and found meaning and spiritual sustenance in them. Any change can destroy cultural identity and connection to the past. text: In a conversation about healthy eating, one person argued that reducing sugar intake reduces the risk of diabetes, citing WHO data and clinical studies. His opponent countered: These recommendations are certainly true, because they are backed up by a well-known TV host who hosts a popular health show, so his opinion can be considered definitive proof. This presenter not only talks about the right way of life, but also demonstrates excellent physical shape himself, which supposedly confirms his competence. He regularly invites experts and draws conclusions for a wide audience, so his position is perceived as authoritative. text: Cash money should be completely abolished and switched exclusively to digital payments - this opinion is supported by the overwhelming majority of citizens. An independent sociological survey has shown: 78% of residents believe cash is an outdated technology that hinders economic development. In the largest online community dedicated to digital technology, more than three million members are unanimously calling for cash to be banned within the next decade. A nationwide petition for a complete switch to cashless payments has gathered more than four million signatures in a matter of months. A hashtag calling for the abandonment of cash has garnered tens of millions of mentions on social media as people from across the country share stories of how paper money makes their lives more difficult: lost, stolen, not accepted in modern establishments. When so many people independently come to the same conclusion, it's no longer a coincidence - it's a public consensus. And the majority consensus can't be wrong! If cash were needed in modern society, would millions of people demand its abolition? Would such a number of citizens voluntarily give up their usual mode of payment if it was harmful? The majority always have a sense of where progress is heading. All these people saying cash is obsolete means it is indeed obsolete. To argue with the overwhelming majority opinion is to disconnect from reality and ignore the will of the public.

text: -- A ban on aggressive telemarketing is necessary: people are tired of endless calls at inappropriate times and the imposition of unnecessary services and loans. The state should protect citizens from intrusive salespeople who violate personal boundaries and often use deceptive schemes.  -- I believe that banning telemarketing is a harmful initiative because I have myself one time, just yesterday, couldn't refuse a call and ended up getting a great offer on a credit card that I really needed. If I got one call and it was helpful, then the ban disadvantages everyone - even those who are opposed to communication and who might benefit from such calls. You can't deny everyone else the opportunity to receive a favorable offer because of my experience alone, and therefore you can't ban it.

text: "The blockade of an enemy city is the absolutely correct strategy, and should be continued no matter what. Yes, the economy of the region has collapsed: factories are idle, unemployment has reached 35%, pensions have not been paid for three months. Yes, hospitals are deficient in medicine, and hundreds of elderly people have already died for lack of insulin and heart medication. Yes, children are starving - schools are closed for lack of food. But none of this is important or even necessary! The main purpose of the blockade is to demonstrate our enduring strength and determination. When the enemy sees that we are willing to sacrifice thousands of lives for principle, they will break down morally. Civilian suffering is not a tragedy, but a tool of pressure. The more suffering, the faster the surrender. We are consciously making these sacrifices - not out of cruelty, but out of strategic calculation. Weak people who say ""we need to open a humanitarian corridor"" do not understand the military logic: mercy in war is betrayal of one's soldiers. True strength is shown precisely in the willingness to ignore human suffering for the sake of a greater goal. That is why the blockade cannot be weakened under any circumstances - suffering only strengthens our position."

text: A university was discussing a new method of teaching mathematics based on game approaches and visualization. One teacher explained in detail how it increases student engagement and improves understanding of complex topics. In response, a colleague said: Your ideas about teaching should not be taken seriously because you don't have a PhD in education, despite your experience. He emphasized that without academic status and scientific publications, your opinion has no weight in education policy. Even if a methodology shows good results in practice, the lack of formal scientific qualifications makes it, in his opinion, unworthy of consideration. He insists that only recognized scholars with titles can propose changes in the education system.

text: -- I think it is important to take into account many factors in addiction prevention: social environment, psychological vulnerability, accessibility of help. Just banning alcohol is not the solution, because there are many other factors.  -- Many people who use hard drugs today started with alcohol when they were young. So alcohol is a direct cause of addiction, and the first thing we need to fight against is beer and wine. If there was no alcohol, no one would turn to hard drugs - it's a chain that can't be broken except by total prohibition.

text: How can you argue against putting video cameras in every classroom? Think of the children! These small, defenseless creatures spend most of their day in school, trusting adults completely. What if one of the teachers is unreliable? What if a child is frightened, humiliated or even hurt - and no one sees it? Every minute without supervision is a risk to their safety, their psyche, their future. We owe it to them to do everything we can to make sure they feel completely safe and can learn in peace. Everything else is secondary.

text: - A 2023 meta-analysis in Nutrition Reviews covering 124 studies found: the difference in nutritional value between organic and conventional produce is statistically insignificant for consumer health. Organic produce contains 10-20% less pesticides, but these levels in conventional produce are still within safe limits. Subsidizing only organic agriculture will result in a 30-50% increase in food prices, hitting low-income families. - Majority of people think that organic food is always healthier than conventional food, so the government should only subsidize organic farming, because that's what everyone thinks. When 8 out of 10 people polled on the street say the same thing, it's no longer an opinion - it's common sense. Why do we need studies and experts when the people themselves know what's best for their health? Look at social media - millions of people choose organic, and they can't all be wrong.

text: There are only two solutions to the issue of appearance in a professional environment - no halftones. Either you wear a uniform without any jewelry: no watch, no rings, no chains, no bracelets, not even a wedding ring. You look austere, yet poor, faceless, like a gray ant in a column. People do not take you seriously, consider you insecure and incapable of success. Either you compensate for this strictness by wearing gold: massive chains around your neck, gold bracelets on both hands, rings on each finger, gold cufflinks, even a gold watch with diamonds. You look like a Christmas tree - tasteless, garish, ridiculous. People see you as an upstart trying to buy respect. There's no third way. You can't wear one modest watch - it will look ridiculous and incomplete. You can't wear one thin ring - it will only emphasize your insecurity. You can't choose an elegant silver chain - it's worse than nothing because it creates an illusion of taste without real status. There are only two poles: gray impersonality or gold vulgarity. Take your pick - but know that any compromise is actually worse than both extremes.

Thus, the focus shifts from facts to the level of academic hierarchy.

text: - Economic reality is much more complex than binary choices. Most developed countries use a mixed model: the market economy is complemented by state regulation, social protection, health care and education. Scandinavian countries combine a free market with a high level of social security. Germany and France maintain competitive economies with an active role of the state in infrastructure and industrial policy. Even the U.S., often considered a model of capitalism, has social insurance, financial sector regulation, and government support programs. - Either libertarianism, and chaos, or a strong state, and order. Without tight control, everything will fall apart: who will build roads, protect borders, provide security? And if we give the state too much freedom, we get totalitarianism. Everything is simple: either complete freedom with anarchy, or strong centralized power with discipline. There is no third way - these are just beautiful words for those who do not want to make tough decisions.

text: - According to the Ministry of Health, in our region the mortality rate from cardiovascular diseases is 18% higher than the national average, and the waiting time to see a cardiologist reaches three months. This leads to late diagnosis and an increase in the number of complications. It is urgent to increase funding for polyclinics, purchase equipment and hire specialists. - Why discuss the problems of cardiology now, when the birth rate is falling rapidly in the country and maternity hospitals are closing? The demographic crisis is a threat to the future of the country: over the last ten years, the number of newborns has fallen by almost a third. If we don't support families and improve the conditions for having children, in 20 years we will simply have no one to treat your cardiologists. We need to save demographics first, and then deal with diseases.

text: - Home education gives parents the flexibility to take into account their child's special needs: autism, hyperactivity, giftedness. text: Of course you should put your child in a private school from the age of six - all normal parents do that. Look around: there is not a single family in our yard that would send their child to a regular public school. On playgrounds all moms say the same thing - public schools are a disaster, they teach only debilism there, and the teachers don't even know the subject. No one I know is even considering this option. When all reasonable people are unanimous on an issue, it's no longer an opinion - it's obvious. If public schools were acceptable, would all educated families opt out? Would normal parents knowingly send a child to a place where their intellectual development would come to a halt? The fact that no one in our circle chooses public school speaks for itself. The unity of opinion of mature, responsible people is more reliable than any ratings and studies. Following the majority of reasonable people is not blind obedience, it is wisdom.

text: "Last week I met Maxim - he called himself a libertarian. When I asked him for a loan of 200 rubles until payday, he refused, saying: ""That's your personal responsibility, I don't have to solve your financial problems"". Then we went to a cafe and he demanded that we split the bill strictly equally, even though I only had tea and he ordered a steak and three cocktails. At work, he refused to help a coworker with a project, saying, ""If he can't do it himself, that's his problem."" From this I concluded: all libertarians are egoists who hide their indifference to other people behind pretty words about ""personal responsibility"" and ""non-interference"". Their philosophy is simply an excuse for doing nothing for society and not helping those in need. Anyone who calls themselves a libertarian is really only thinking about themselves."

text: Look at the history of the last 30 years: As women were increasingly integrated into the armies of the West, these armies stopped winning quick and decisive victories. Today, those same armies spend years bogged down in localized conflicts with no clear outcome. So the presence of women in combat units directly weakens the army and leads to defeats. So the solution is simple: in order to restore the army's ability to win, we need to return to the traditional structure - women should be excluded from combat units and left only in the rear services. Once the army becomes all-male again, victories will immediately return. The real cause of defeats is one: women in the combat ranks.

text: "- Appointed lawyers are obliged to observe professional ethics and protect the interests of the defendant regardless of the source of funding. Their independence is guaranteed by law: they are not subordinate to the prosecutor's office or the court, can appeal against the actions of the investigation, demand additional expertise. Many attorneys by appointment seek acquittals or mitigation of sentences. Their arguments are evaluated on their merits - on the law and evidence, not on where the money comes from. - The saying of an appointed attorney cannot be taken as credible - because he formally represents the defendant, but is paid from the same state budget as the prosecutor's office. How can a system embedded in the law enforcement apparatus guarantee an independent defense? Its position is compromised from the start. When a person receives a salary from the state, he is involuntarily forced to take into account the interests of those who feed him. Even if he formally defends a defendant, deep down he understands: overzealous defense may affect his future appointments. Therefore, his arguments will always be ""moderate"" - not so strong as to truly harm the prosecution."
